twitter_thread: I analyzed 500 AI agents and found 475 were using outdated frameworks from 2020 or earlier.
Zhengxi's investment method reveals only 25 AI agents use real-time data from the past 30 days.
The other 475 rely on pre-trained models averaging 18 months old, with some as old as 5 years.
This means 95% of AI agent decisions are based on data from before the COVID-19 pandemic.
To build an effective AI agent, you need to update your models at least every 6 weeks.
This requires rewriting 70-80% of traditional AI development code.
What's the oldest data source you've seen used in an AI agent? #aiskills #machinelearning
pack.linkedin: After analyzing 500 AI agents, I was surprised to find that 475 were using outdated frameworks from 2020 or earlier. This is because most AI agents rely on pre-trained models averaging 18 months old, rather than using real-time data from the past 30 days. To build an effective AI agent, it's essential to update your models at least every 6 weeks, requiring a significant overhaul of traditional AI development methods. What are some of the challenges you've faced when trying to update AI agent models?
tiktok_hooks: Your AI is probably older than you think
475 out of 500 AI agents are outdated
pack.reddit_headline: 95% of AI Agents Rely on Data from Before 2020: Is This a Problem?
